children.heading: Inferences from any law or treaty
children.label: a Inferences from any law or treaty

children.text: from any provision of law (whether or not in effect before ), including any provision contained in any appropriation Act, unless such provision specifically authorizes the introduction of United States Armed Forces into hostilities or into such situations and states that it is intended to constitute specific statutory authorization within the meaning of this chapter; orNovember 7, 1973

from any treaty heretofore or hereafter ratified unless such treaty is implemented by legislation specifically authorizing the introduction of United States Armed Forces into hostilities or into such situations and stating that it is intended to constitute specific statutory authorization within the meaning of this chapter.

children.heading: Joint headquarters operations of high-level military commands
children.label: b Joint headquarters operations of high-level military commands
children.text: Nothing in this chapter shall be construed to require any further specific statutory authorization to permit members of United States Armed Forces to participate jointly with members of the armed forces of one or more foreign countries in the headquarters operations of high-level military commands which were established prior to , and pursuant to the United Nations Charter or any treaty ratified by the United States prior to such date.November 7, 1973

children.heading: Introduction of United States Armed Forces
children.label: c Introduction of United States Armed Forces
children.text: For purposes of this chapter, the term “introduction of United States Armed Forces” includes the assignment of members of such armed forces to command, coordinate, participate in the movement of, or accompany the regular or irregular military forces of any foreign country or government when such military forces are engaged, or there exists an imminent threat that such forces will become engaged, in hostilities.

children.heading: Constitutional authorities or existing treaties unaffected; construction against grant of Presidential authority respecting use of United States Armed Forces
children.label: d Constitutional authorities or existing treaties unaffected; construction against grant of Presidential authority respecting use of United States Armed Forces

children.text: is intended to alter the constitutional authority of the Congress or of the President, or the provisions of existing treaties; or

shall be construed as granting any authority to the President with respect to the introduction of United States Armed Forces into hostilities or into situations wherein involvement in hostilities is clearly indicated by the circumstances which authority he would not have had in the absence of this chapter.
